title: Waaree Sets Up 3,000 MW Solar Facility In Gujarat - BW Sustainabilityworld
content: New manufacturing unit strengthens domestic solar capacity amid rising demand and import substitution push Waaree Energies on Monday said its wholly owned subsidiary, Sangam Solar One, has commissioned a solar module manufacturing facility in Gujarat with an annual capacity of 3,000 megawatt (MW), strengthening its presence in India's renewable energy sector. The facility comprises four solar module production lines, marking a significant scale-up in the company's domestic manufacturing capabilities. The expansion comes at a time when demand for solar power is rising and India is seeking to strengthen local manufacturing. Capacity Expansion The new unit adds to Waaree Energies' manufacturing footprint and is aimed at supporting the country's growing renewable energy requirements. The development is aligned with efforts to reduce dependence on imports and build a stronger domestic supply chain for solar equipment. Waaree Energies operates across the solar value chain, including manufacturing solar photovoltaic (PV) modules and providing engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) services, along with power generation and related solutions. The company offers a range of solar products, including mono PERC, bifacial and polycrystalline modules. It also provides inverters and energy storage solutions as part of its broader renewable energy portfolio. The addition of the Gujarat facility is expected to support the scaling up of solar infrastructure in India, as the country continues to expand its renewable energy capacity to meet rising electricity demand and climate goals.
